files.contents:
{
  "name": "express-tailwind-color-app",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Express + Tailwind CSS starter for Hello World color app",
  "scripts": {
    "build:css": "tailwindcss -i ./src/styles/input.css -o ./public/styles.css",
    "build": "tsc && npm run build:css",
    "dev": "npm run build:css && ts-node src/server.ts",
    "start": "node dist/server.js",
    "test": "mocha -r ts-node/register tests/**/*.test.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.19.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/mocha": "^10.0.6",
    "@types/node": "^20.11.30",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.19",
    "mocha": "^10.4.0",
    "postcss": "^8.4.35",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.9",
    "ts-node": "^10.9.2",
    "typescript": "^5.4.5"
  }
}
Hello World

# Express + Tailwind CSS Starter

This project initializes an Express server with Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and Autoprefixer.

Prerequisites:
- Node.js 18+

Setup:
1) Install dependencies
   npm install

2) Build TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
   npm run build

3) Start the server
   npm start

During development (no manual compile step; CSS is built once):
   npm run dev

Run tests:
   npm test

Project structure:
- src/        TypeScript server and styles
- public/     Static assets served by Express (styles.css is generated here)
- dist/       Compiled JavaScript output (generated by TSC)

Notes:
- Tailwind CSS is configured via tailwind.config.cjs and postcss.config.cjs.
- CSS entry is src/styles/input.css; output is public/styles.css.
- The server serves static files from the public directory and a basic index.html at '/'.

notes: Run npm install to fetch dependencies, then npm run build and npm start.
Tests verify that required dependencies are declared in package.json.
